items.headline: Algarve Apartments added to development tracker
items.summary: Algarve Apartments in San Jose (Approved) has been added to the development tracker. Scale: 91 affordable units, 7 stories.

items.headline: Historic property mixed-use project continued to July
items.summary: A resource alteration permit for 434 Crescent Avenue, involving demolition or significant modification of a historic single-family home and construction of four three-story townhomes on the rear of the lot, was continued to the July 1, 2026 Heritage Preservation Commission meeting. The project requires review because of the existing structure's historic status.

items.headline: Los Gatos schedules municipal election for November 3, 2026
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution calling a general municipal election for November 3, 2026, for three seats.

items.headline: Final map approved for Tract 10675 Boulevard development
items.summary: The council adopted a resolution approving the final map for Tract 10675 located on Boulevard, completing a subdivision or development phase.

items.headline: Council confirms 2026 weed abatement assessment report
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution confirming the 2026 Weed Abatement Assessment Report. Property owners assessed for weed abatement costs should verify their assessment details.

items.headline: 68-unit apartment complex permitted on East St John Street
items.summary: A $10 million apartment building with integrated parking garage is cleared to build at 700 E St John St. This is the only multifamily housing project in this batch, adding 68 units to the rental stock.

items.headline: Variance approved for single-family home addition with reduced setback
items.summary: A homeowner on an unspecified street sought permission to add 730 square feet to a one-story home, bringing it to 2,592 square feet total. The Planning Commission granted a variance allowing a 7-foot-4-inch rear yard setback instead of the required 10 feet, and permitted 34 percent rear yard encroachment where code normally caps it at 25 percent.

items.headline: Parks and Recreation Strategic Plan adopted
items.summary: The City Council adopted a new Parks and Recreation Strategic Plan on May 26. The plan sets direction for parks and recreation services in Mountain View.
